based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/external/pytorch.sgd-momentum.multi-step.golden.jsonl. v0.9.2 classical PyTorch-style SGD momentum multi-step adversarial plate.

mutation.explanation: Receipts[0] declares nesterov=true; receipts[1] drops the nesterov flag (defaults to false = classical). Rule 26 catches the inconstancy: nesterov is in sgd_momentum's constancy key list (set up in v0.9.2 anticipating v0.9.3). A training run cannot switch optimizer variants mid-stream — Sutskever 2013 / PyTorch convention assumes consistent optimizer config across steps (LR is the only legitimately-scheduled hyperparameter).

reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 26: Multi-step optimizer-config inconstancy — receipts[1].optimizer_config.nesterov differs from receipts[0] (dropped from true → absent/false).
purpose: v0.9.2 momentum multi-step anti-circularity fixture (kind: 'nesterov-flag-inconstancy'). Pressure-tests that the named multi-step rule fires BEFORE the reconciler consults fixture_status metadata.
v0_9_2_trust_framing: Rule 25 (multi-step optimizer-state chain — momentum buffer chain) is a STRUCTURAL consistency check. Producer-authenticity caveat per Rule 17 / Fang et al. 2023 applies.
